subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: SIRT1 physically interacts with the RelA/p65 subunit of NF-kappaB and inhibits transcription by deacetylating RelA/p65 at lysine 310.
rationale: SIRT1 deacetylates RelA/p65 -- same axis, opposite sign

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Here, we firstly demonstrate an inverse correlation between AKT acetylation and phosphorylation modulated by SIRT1 in MMe cells treated with CO-338.
rationale: AKT acetylation modulated by SIRT1 -- modification axis matches

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Here, we firstly demonstrate an inverse correlation between AKT acetylation and phosphorylation modulated by SIRT1 in MMe cells treated with PARP1 inhibitor.
rationale: AKT acetylation modulated by SIRT1 -- modification axis matches

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Although SIRT1 dependent acetylation of CREB activity has been reported (), CCAR2 was not previously known to be involved in this regulatory axis.
rationale: SIRT1 dependent acetylation of CREB

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Blockade of AR improves the NAD + levels by rescuing the NAMPT biosynthesis pathway, improving Sirt-1 activity mediated deacetylation of Egr, and subsequent induction of Egr-1 target genes during hyperglycemia (XREF_FIG).
rationale: Sirt-1 deacetylates Egr -- same axis, opposite sign

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Second, Sirt1 inhibition may inhibit FoxO binding and deacetylation either directly or via inhibition of p300 and CBP binding and deacetylation thereby activating FoxO mediated responses (such activation of caspases) leading to apoptosis of cancer cells.
rationale: Sirt1 normally promotes FoxO deacetylation, opposite of Acetylation

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Oxidative stress stimuli triggered the translocation of FOXO from the cytoplasm to the nucleus and FOXO acetylation by SIRT1 that activates FOXO transcriptional activity.
rationale: FOXO acetylation by SIRT1 -- modification axis matches, sign matches

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Counter-regulation of histone acetylation by SIRT1 occurs in a variety of cell types ( xref – xref ).
rationale: SIRT1 counter-regulates histone acetylation, indicating opposite sign

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Counter regulation of histone acetylation by SIRT1 occurs in a variety of cell types.
rationale: counter regulation of histone acetylation by SIRT1 implies deacetylation, opposite sign

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Our findings were further substantiated using pharmacological inhibitors of SIRT1 (sirtinol, EX-527), which enhanced histone H3 acetylation on lysine 9 (H3K9Ac), another well-known SIRT1 substrate, and engendered similar level of SA-betaGal stained cells (XREF_SUPPLEMENTARY).
rationale: SIRT1 inhibitors enhanced H3 acetylation, meaning SIRT1 deacetylates H3

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Our findings were further substantiated using pharmacological inhibitors of SIRT1 (sirtinol, EX-527), which enhanced histone H3 acetylation on lysine 9 (H3K9Ac), another well-known SIRT1 substrate, and engendered similar level of SA-betaGal stained cells (XREF_SUPPLEMENTARY).
rationale: SIRT1 inhibitors enhanced H3 acetylation, meaning SIRT1 deacetylates H3

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Depletion of SIRT1 in CIN612 cells increased the global acetylation of H1 (Lys26) and H4 (Lys16) and this was most pronounced in undifferentiated cells, where the levels of total acetylated H1 (Lys26) and H4 (Lys16) are normally low.
rationale: depletion of SIRT1 increased acetylation of H4, meaning SIRT1 deacetylates H4

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Depletion of SIRT1 in CIN612 cells increased the global acetylation of H1 (Lys26) and H4 (Lys16) and this was most pronounced in undifferentiated cells, where the levels of total acetylated H1 (Lys26) and H4 (Lys16) are normally low.
rationale: depletion of SIRT1 increased acetylation of H4, meaning SIRT1 deacetylates H4

subject: SIRT1

rationale: Depletion of SIRT1 increased acetylation of H4, meaning SIRT1 deacetylates

subject: SIRT1
evidence_text: Furthermore, Sirt1 and HNF-1alpha co-localize on two HNF-1alpha binding sites on the Crp promoter, leading to decreased acetylation of lysine 16 of histone H4 at these sites only in response to nutrient restriction.
rationale: Sirt1 leads to decreased acetylation of histone H4 -- opposite sign

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Induced expression and/or activity of RTKs, fibroblast growth factor receptor (FGFR) 1-3, EGFR, and IGF1R were detected by blot in OVCAR5 and SKOV3 in response to JQ1 treatment that was accompanied by a dose dependent increase in downstream P70S6 kinase, AKT, and RAF-MEK-ERK activity.
rationale: AKT and RTK are both activated by JQ1, no direct relation asserted

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Taken collectively, our data may explain AKT activation in those tumors that display amplification/activation of RTKs but have normal PI3K/PTEN levels.
rationale: co-occurrence of AKT activation and RTK amplification, no relation asserted

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Overall, RTK/ACK1 signaling nexus explains alternate modes of AKT activation in those tumors that display amplification/activation of RTKs and non-receptor tyrosine kinases and therefore uncovers a new arsenal in form of ACK1 inhibitors.
rationale: evidence states RTK activates AKT, not AKT activates RTK

subject: AKT
evidence_text: ShcA down-regulation also exerted a milder decrease in basal Akt activation (XREF_FIG), consistent with the proposed role of Shc in insulin- and growth factor induced PI3K activation [XREF_BIBR, XREF_BIBR, XREF_BIBR].
rationale: evidence asserts SHC activates AKT, not AKT activates SHC

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Akt promotes glucose uptake for glycolysis and ATP production by inducing the plasma membrane localization of glucose transporters, such as Glut1 and Glut4 ( - ).
rationale: Akt induces plasma membrane localization of glucose transporters -- different axes

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Akt induces glucose uptake by inducing glucose transporter expression and membrane localization, and enhances glycolytic activity by activating hexokinase ( ).
rationale: claim is activity but evidence is amount and localization

subject: AKT
evidence_text: It is known that the phosphoinositide 3-kinase /Akt pathway induces expression of glucose transporters and stimulates the activities of hexokinase and phospho-fructokinase.
rationale: claim is activity but evidence is amount (induces expression)

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Akt increases glucose uptake by enhancing transcription and membrane translocation of glucose transporters.
rationale: claim is activity but evidence is transcription and translocation

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Interestingly, Akt also enhances cellular glucose uptake by mobilizing glucose transporters to the cell surface [ ].
rationale: claim is activity but evidence is localization (mobilizing to cell surface)

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Similarly, because the PI3K-Akt pathway can potentiate fibrogenic SMAD signals in cells responding to TGFbeta 44, it is possible that GIV and its GEF function enhance SMAD signals via activation of Akt.
rationale: Akt pathway can potentiate fibrogenic SMAD signals

subject: AKT
evidence_text: We found that the biphasic induction of miR-192 expression by TGF-beta in mouse renal glomerular mesangial cells initially involved the Smad transcription factors, followed by sustained expression that was promoted by acetylation of the transcription factor Ets-1 and histone H3 by the acetyltransferase p300, which was activated by the serine and threonine kinase Akt.
rationale: Akt and Smad are mentioned but no relation connects them

subject: AKT
evidence_text: These results strongly suggest that riluzole, by successively inhibiting AKT and activating GSK3 activities, increases Smad linker phosphorylation.
rationale: claim is activity but evidence is modification (phosphorylation)

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Recent evidence shows that the noncanonical Smad signaling, such as the p38MAPK and the PI3K and AKT pathways, can crosstalk and interact with the canonical TGFbeta and Smad signaling during EMT process XREF_BIBR, XREF_BIBR.
rationale: evidence states pathways interact (binding axis), claim is activity

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Hepatic Akt activation induces marked hypoglycemia, hepatomegaly, and hypertriglyceridemia with sterol regulatory element binding protein involvement.
rationale: SREBF involvement is mentioned but no direct relation asserted

subject: AKT
evidence_text: Hepatic Akt activation induces marked hypoglycemia, hepatomegaly, and hypertriglyceridemia with sterol regulatory element binding protein involvement.
rationale: Akt activation induces phenotypes with SREBF involvement, no relation asserted

evidence_text: The human 9-1-1 complex interacts with hTDG.
rationale: 9-1-1 complex interacts with hTDG -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: The interaction between hTDG and the 9-1-1 complex was also demonstrated by co-immunoprecipitation (Co-IP).
rationale: interaction between hTDG and the 9-1-1 complex

evidence_text: Two serine phosphorylation sites in the C-terminus of Rad9 are critical for 9-1-1 binding to TopBP1 and activation of the DNA damage checkpoint response in HeLa cells.
rationale: 9-1-1 binding to TopBP1 -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: When assembled, 9-1-1 binds to TopBP1 and activates the ATR-Chk1 checkpoint pathway.
rationale: 9-1-1 binds to TopBP1 -- binding axis matches

rationale: 9-1-1 binds to TopBP1 -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: RHINO interacts with Rad9-Rad1-Hus1 (9-1-1) complex and with ATR activator TopBP1, which recruit it to the site of DNA damage.
rationale: RHINO interacts with 9-1-1 complex -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: The interaction of RHINO with 9-1-1 was of sufficient affinity to allow for the purification of a stable heterotetrameric RHINO-Rad9-Hus1-Rad1 complex in vitro.
rationale: interaction of RHINO with 9-1-1 -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: We show that 9-1-1 also binds and stimulates FEN1.
rationale: 9-1-1 binds FEN1 -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: Examining the nature of 9-1-1 interaction with DNA ligase I, we show that there is a similar degree of stimulation on ligation substrates with different structures, and that there is specificity for DNA ligase I. 9-1-1 improves the binding of DNA ligase I to nicked double strand DNA.
rationale: 9-1-1 interaction with DNA ligase I -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: Rad9 and 9-1-1 also interact with human MSH2, MSH3, and MSH6.
rationale: 9-1-1 interacts with MSH2 -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: Rad9 and 9-1-1 also interact with human MSH2, MSH3, and MSH6.
rationale: 9-1-1 interacts with MSH3 -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: Rad9 and 9-1-1 also interact with human MSH2, MSH3, and MSH6.
rationale: 9-1-1 interacts with MSH6 -- binding axis matches

evidence_text: It seems as if the interaction between Rad17 and the 9-1-1 complex is crucial not only for the chromatin loading of the 9-1-1 complex but also for the signaling from ATR to Rad17, however, the identification of the KYxxL motif has revealed that the interaction with the 9-1-1 complex is dispensable for UV induced Rad17 phosphorylation.
rationale: interaction between Rad17 and the 9-1-1 complex -- binding axis matches

subject: ABL_family
evidence_text: Translocations that create BCR-ABL1 and other human leukemogenic ABL gene fusions consistently retain the ABL SH3 and SH2 domains, despite the fact that biochemical and structure data have established that these domains restrain kinase activity.
rationale: no binding relation between ABL and ABL asserted

subject: ABL_family
evidence_text: Whereas imatinib and ponatinib bind potently to both the DDR and ABL kinases, the hydrophobic interactions of the ABL P-loop appear poorly satisfied by DDR1-IN-1 suggesting a structural basis for its DDR1 selectivity.
rationale: ABL and DDR are parallel targets of drugs, no interaction asserted

subject: ABL_family
evidence_text: It is likely that ABL1 kinase can be recruited to NRP1-containing multiprotein complexes through other interactions that may be involve integrins, as NRP1 interacts with integrins ( xref , xref ), and integrins interact with ABL kinases ( xref ).
rationale: integrins interact with ABL kinases -- binding axis matches

subject: ABL_family
evidence_text: Among over 170 kinases measured by KiNativ assays, six other proteins, p38, EphA2, EphB4, MAP3K1, FYN, and ZAK have binding affinities ≤100 nM, seven targets, JNK, MAP2K5, ABL, CSK, GCN2, IRAK1, and SRC have binding affinity between 290–1,000 nM ( Table S2 ).
rationale: JNK and ABL are in a parallel list of targets

subject: ABL_family
evidence_text: Taken together, our data demonstrate the positive role of BOC in neuronal differentiation of P19 EC cells and cortical NPCs, and BOC interacts with ABL, resulting in JNK activation and promotion of neuronal differentiation.
rationale: claim is binding but evidence is activity (JNK activation)

subject: ABL_family
evidence_text: It has been found that Notch interacts genetically with ABL , as Notch and ABL mutations synergise to cause synthetic lethality in Drosophila axons [ xref ].
rationale: genetic interaction is not a physical binding relation

subject: ABL_family
evidence_text: We show that the p85 alpha subunit of PI-3 kinase associates with BCR and ABL and that transient expression of BCR and ABL in fibroblasts and down-regulation of BCR and ABL expression using antisense oligodeoxynucleotides (ODNs) in Ph1 cells activates and inhibits, respectively, PI-3 kinase enzymatic activity.
rationale: p85 alpha subunit of PI-3 kinase associates with ABL
